penalties.description: Video appears to be educational content rather than a complete FLS Task 2 performance, preventing verification of task completion.

consensus_score.reasoning: Major disagreement between models on video content interpretation. Claude identified this as educational content without complete task performance, while GPT-4o scored it as a completed task. Given the fundamental disagreement about whether a scorable task performance occurred, the conservative approach is to treat this as incomplete educational content that cannot be scored according to FLS rubric requirements.

disagreements.rationale: Fundamental disagreement about whether video shows complete task performance versus educational content. Conservative approach requires clear task completion evidence.

claude_value.description: Cannot determine if the marked circle was completely cut out as the video shows educational content rather than a complete task performance.

gpt_value.description: The cut deviated from the marked line on the circle.

disagreements.rationale: If the video content is ambiguous about task completion, incomplete_circle is the appropriate penalty rather than line_deviation which assumes completion occurred.
Conservative approach given disagreement about task completion - only include phases that are clearly observable.
Follows from incomplete task determination - no score can be assigned without clear task completion.
